# Change the role of this /README.md to describe the generation experiment not the code generated.

## Context

- The usual role for a README.md in a repo is to provide the entry point for a human
  to read about the *contents* of the repo.
- However the *code" in this repo was auto generated by cursor, and that changes the role of a 
  useful README.md
- I.e. it should talk about the auto generation process, artefacts, and objectives.


## Instructions

- Replace the current README contents completely with material as described below, plus any content you think also belongs conceptually.

- It is a code generation experiment
- It depends on providing and using skills as desribed below
- It depends on a set of strictly ordered prompts as described below
- The experiment has been very successful - with it generating a good quality code base, that
  required zero human editing.
- The current status (very encouraging experimental results - not proven production code)

## Providing and using skills

Cursor Skills were written and registered to influence various generic and hopefully reusable, good engineering practices. For example:

-  Dependency injection via interfaces at system boundaries
-  Deterministic fake test double implementations for those interfaces to use in tests
-  Code structure to support separation-of-concerns and readability
-  How to refactor
-  How to apply intelligent renaming of functions, types, files etc.
-  Test isolation
-  Test rigour
-  Test uniformity
-  Test writing (patterns)
-  Linting requirements for acceptance

The set of skills registered can be seen at https://github.com/peterhoward42/cursorlib.git

## Strictly ordered prompts

- A sequence of prompts were used in a strictly ordered sequence aiming to:
    -  Encourage the key shapes and patterns in the code generation to take root and
       form a precedent early one
    -  Package the work into relatively small packages to give the LLM small domains in which to get things right first time.

- The prompts were written in advance and included in the repo as examples and to form a record.
- Each prompt was issued in a new, dedicated chat context to conserve LLM memory resources and prevent precision drift from resource exhaustion.    


## High level complete system spec overview

 A conceptual entry point and fallback reference was provided via a high level overview of the complete generated system behaviour.
